mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0% within a single month, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving immediate positive momentum in equity prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which increases corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observed periods, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained manufacturing capacity, any further decline in the industrial production index triggers supply-side inflation fears that compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 50% of triggered episodes, or the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income, proxied by increases in personal savings and financial assets, directly fuels consumer expenditure and drives positive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth, or the rank correlation coefficient drops below 0.02 across all sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to suppress corporate investment and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5%, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and contracting consumer credit balances indicates a shift from debt-fueled to income-fueled consumption, which stabilizes corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/RSAFS signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 20-day returns, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index erodes real purchasing power and triggers immediate risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered CPIAUCSL spikes, or the signal shows no directional consistency across multiple inflationary episodes.
